session.assistant:
The `ERK_PLAN_BACKEND` env var is a separate concept from the Python `plan_backend` property. The workflow files use `plan_backend` as an input name that maps to `ERK_PLAN_BACKEND`. The plan only asks us to rename the Python-level `plan_backend` → `pr_backend`. The workflow inputs (`plan_backend`) are GitHub Actions interface items and the plan doesn't mention them.
Now let me launch Wave 1 (source files) in parallel. I'll break the 43 source Python files into batches:
**Source files:** - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/context.py` - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/helpers.py` - `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/learn/tracking.py` - `src/erk/core/pr_context_provider.py` - `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/shared.py` (already modified) - `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/view/operation.py` - `src/erk/cli/commands/reconcile_pipeline.py` - `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/metadata_helpers.py` - `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/rewrite_cmd.py` - `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py` - `src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot_remote_dispatch.py` - `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/dispatch_cmd.py` - `src/erk/cli/commands/objective_helpers.py` - `src/erk/cli/commands/learn/learn_cmd.py` - `src/erk/cli/commands/land_cmd.py` - `src/erk/cli/commands/land_learn.py` - `src/erk/cli/commands/land_pipeline.py` - `src/erk/cli/commands/land_stack.py` - `src/erk/cli/commands/implement_shared.py` - Plus 15 exec/scripts files

## Summary

I've completed the renaming task in `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/reconcile_pipeline.py`:

**Changes made:**
- Line 107: Renamed `ctx.plan_backend` to `ctx.pr_backend`

**Note:** This file contains only one occurrence of `plan_backend` (at line 107). There are no imports of `require_plan_backend` in this file, and no other `plan_backend` references to rename.

The change has been verified and the file now correctly uses `ctx.pr_backend.resolve_pr_number_for_branch()` instead of the old `ctx.plan_backend` reference.

## Summary

I have renamed the property `plan_backend` to `pr_backend` in `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/context.py`.

### Changes Made:

1. **Property definition (line 176)**: Changed `def plan_backend(self)` to `def pr_backend(self)`

The property provides access to `plan_store` as a `ManagedPrBackend` read/write interface. The implementation and docstring remain unchanged, with only the property name itself being updated to the new `pr_backend` identifier.

<output> [Tool: Read] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/helpers.py"} [Tool: Read] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/helpers.py"} [Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/helpers.py","old_string":"def require_plan_backend(ctx: click.Context) -> ManagedPrBackend:
 """Get ManagedPrBackend from context, exiting with error if not initialized.

 Uses LBYL pattern to check context before accessing.

 Args:
 ctx: Click context (must have ErkContext in ctx.obj)

 Returns:
 ManagedPrBackend instance from context

 Raises:
 SystemExit: If context not initialized (exits with code 1)

 Example:
 >>> @click.command()
 >>> @click.pass_context
 >>> def my_command(ctx: click.Context) -> None:
 ... backend = require_plan_backend(ctx)
 ... result = backend.create_managed_pr(repo_root, title, content, labels, metadata)
 """
 if ctx.obj is None:
 click.echo("Error: Context not initialized", err=True)
 raise SystemExit(1)

 return ctx.obj.plan_backend","new_string":"def require_pr_backend(ctx: click.Context) -> ManagedPrBackend:
 """Get ManagedPrBackend from context, exiting with error if not initialized.

 Uses LBYL pattern to check context before accessing.

 Args:
 ctx: Click context (must have ErkContext in ctx.obj)

 Returns:
 ManagedPrBackend instance from context

 Raises:
 SystemExit: If context not initialized (exits with code 1)

 Example:
 >>> @click.command()
 >>> @click.pass_context
 >>> def my_command(ctx: click.Context) -> None:
 ... backend = require_pr_backend(ctx)
 ... result = backend.create_managed_pr(repo_root, title, content, labels, metadata)
 """
 if ctx.obj is None:
 click.echo("Error: Context not initialized", err=True)
 raise SystemExit(1)

 return ctx.obj.pr_backend"} [Tool: Edit] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/helpers.py","old_string":"def require_plan_backend(ctx: click.Context) -> ManagedPrBackend:
 """Get ManagedPrBackend from context, exiting with error if not initialized.

 Uses LBYL pattern to check context before accessing.

 Args:
 ctx: Click context (must have ErkContext in ctx.obj)

 Returns:
 ManagedPrBackend instance from context

 Raises:
 SystemExit: If context not initialized (exits with code 1)

 Example:
 >>> @click.command()
 >>> @click.pass_context
 >>> def my_command(ctx: click.Context) -> None:
 ... backend = require_plan_backend(ctx)
 ... result = backend.create_managed_pr(repo_root, title, content, labels, metadata)
 """
 if ctx.obj is None:
 click.echo("Error: Context not initialized", err=True)
 raise SystemExit(1)

 return ctx.obj.plan_backend","new_string":"def require_pr_backend(ctx: click.Context) -> ManagedPrBackend:
 """Get ManagedPrBackend from context, exiting with error if not initialized.

 Uses LBYL pattern to check context before accessing.

 Args:
 ctx: Click context (must have ErkContext in ctx.obj)

 Returns:
 ManagedPrBackend instance from context

 Raises:
 SystemExit: If context not initialized (exits with code 1)

 Example:
 >>> @click.command()
 >>> @click.pass_context
 >>> def my_command(ctx: click.Context) -> None:
 ... backend = require_pr_backend(ctx)
 ... result = backend.create_managed_pr(repo_root, title, content, labels, metadata)
 """
 if ctx.obj is None:
 click.echo("Error: Context not initialized", err=True)
 raise SystemExit(1)

 return ctx.obj.pr_backend"} [Tool: Read] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/helpers.py","offset":397,"limit":30} [Tool: Read] {"file_path":"/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/helpers.py","offset":397,"limit":30}
